title: papaver lapponicum subsp. orientale
intro: Papaver lapponicum subsp. orientale, also known as the Arctic poppy or Finnish poppy, is a subspecies of the Papaver lapponicum plant, which is a perennial herb native to the Arctic regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Papaver lapponicum subsp. orientale include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as promoting wound healing and soothing digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
